orchestrator.terminal_condition: Continue until the objective metric reaches target at 16 of 16 with every active anti-goal held, the human stops or changes the frame, or a blocking flag requires human resolution.

dkrs.decision_target: Reconcile the current PR into keep, reshape, and remove decisions without losing proven behavior.
dkrs.stop_rule: Stop when every public concept and lifecycle workaround in the current PR has a source-backed disposition or return an unresolved checkpoint.

dkrs.decision_target: Decide whether controller(flow).exec with per-exec tags and a current-owned session resource fully models one activation.
dkrs.stop_rule: Stop after executable isolation, nested reuse, close-without-finish, checkpoint, and resume probes compare the current and nearest-context ownership options.

dkrs.decision_target: Choose the minimum reusable Lite structured-cancellation behavior.
dkrs.stop_rule: Stop when scalar, streaming, error, consumer-break, and nested-close probes decide signal-only versus signal plus public cancel without adding start or spawn speculatively.

dkrs.decision_target: Choose the safe context observation projection and terminal trace model.
dkrs.stop_rule: Stop when session, activation, work, channel, role, tool, parentage, terminal outcome, sink flush, and forbidden-value probes identify the smallest extension change.

dkrs.decision_target: Prove bounded queue workers with controller(flow), per-exec tags, and awaited composition before considering start or spawn.
dkrs.stop_rule: Stop when bounded concurrency, backpressure, lease, acknowledge, reject, graceful shutdown, and one activation per delivery pass or a named contract proves a missing primitive.

dkrs.decision_target: Define the evidence, independent verification, publication, and public scope-seam contracts for the issue-triage example.
dkrs.stop_rule: Stop with replayable schemas and denial fixtures for unsupported, stale, unsafe, unauthorized, and duplicate publication cases.
